name: Broadcast / Campaign
description: The unit of work: targeting criteria + message text + delivery settings. One broadcast reaches many recipients across multiple channels simultaneously.
core_concepts: SmsGroup (broadcast) = targeting scope + message + send-time
SmsLog = one per recipient per broadcast
Multi-channel: SMS, Email, Voice, E-boks as parallel delivery paths
Test mode: AlwaysTestMode ProfileRole — sends only to designated test number
Address restriction: profile-level filter on permitted send scope
Merge fields: {{name}}, {{address}} substituted at send time
design_decisions_for_green_ai: Multi-channel as first-class concept from day 1 — not an afterthought
Name: Broadcast, not SmsGroup
TestMode: profile-level flag (not per-broadcast) aligns with ProfileRole model
not_to_copy: SmsGroup naming, SP-heavy send path, duplicated status tables per channel

name: Address Management
description: Local copy of Nordic address registers (DK BBR/OIS, NO Matrikkelen). Addresses organized in a hierarchy: Country→Region→Municipality→Street→Address. Powers both targeting and validation.
core_concepts: Address hierarchy: Country / Region / Municipality / Street / Address
KVHX = Danish address reference (KommuneKode + VejKode + HusNummer + etage/side)
Nightly import pipeline: external feed → normalize → diff → upsert
AddressCorrections: post-import corrections from official errata
Profile address restrictions: a profile is ALLOWED a subset of addresses
design_decisions_for_green_ai: Maintain own copy vs query external APIs on demand? (affects latency and data freshness)
Country scope for v1? (DK only = simple, DK+NO = complex)
Address restriction model: KVHX list? geo-polygon? municipality IDs?
Import = Azure Batch, Azure Function, or cron?
Score too low (0.58) — needs dedicated discovery pass before phase 3 planning
not_to_copy: KVHX string hardcoded throughout, tight coupling between address import and SmsLog, AddressImportKvhxLog SP naming

name: Owner / Recipient Lookup
description: For each address in broadcast scope, resolve who currently lives or works there. Different registries per country. Returns phone numbers and/or email addresses for delivery.
core_concepts: CPR lookup (Denmark): resident by CPR → phone from KRR cross-reference
CVR lookup (Denmark): company → contact phone
NFS lookup (Norway): national property/resident registry
KRR lookup (Norway): digital contact preference + opt-out register
KRR filter: reserved status MUST be respected — skip these recipients
Resolution strategies run in priority order: AttachPhone → AttachEmail → AttachEboks
design_decisions_for_green_ai: Legally most sensitive part — GDPR + consent laws differ per country
External API deps: KRR, NFS, CPR have SLAs, rate limits, costs
Circuit breaker pattern mandatory on all external lookup APIs
NorwayKRRLookup must be gated by ProfileRole — not all operators authorized
Score too low (0.54) — needs dedicated discovery pass per country before phase 3 planning
not_to_copy: Country-specific lookup mixed into generic MessageService, hard-coded priority order, no circuit breaker

name: Filtering / Compliance
description: Filter chain applied to each resolved recipient before delivery. Ensures legal compliance, data quality, and contract constraints.
core_concepts: KRR filter: reserved in national registry → remove (mandatory for NorwayKRRLookup customers)
Positive list: profile has opted-in list → ONLY send to people on that list
Duplicate filter: same phone/email in broadcast → send only once
Address restriction filter: recipient outside allowed scope → skip
Email2SMS whitelist: which email senders can trigger SMS via email gateway
RejectedSmsLog: records WHY recipient was filtered (compliance audit)
design_decisions_for_green_ai: Filter chain: pipeline pattern (ordered, each step can short-circuit)
RejectedSmsLog: store filter reason as typed enum for GDPR erasure compatibility
Positive list import: CSV from customer or API sync — design as extensible
Score too low (0.48) — needs discovery pass before phase 2 positive list planning
not_to_copy: Positive list as flat address table without index, filter logic scattered across multiple SPs

name: Delivery / Channel Routing
design_decisions_for_green_ai: Channels in scope for v1: SMS + Email minimum viable product
ICarrierClient<TChannel> abstraction — swap carriers without rewriting handlers
Dead-letter pattern for permanently failed messages
Fake carrier adapters for tests — never require live credentials in unit tests
not_to_copy: Direct Infobip SDK calls without abstraction, SmsLog and EmailMessage as separate tables for same concept

name: Message Templates
description: Profile-scoped reusable message templates with merge field placeholders. Per-channel variants: SMS text, Email HTML, E-boks formal structure. QuickResponse triggers auto-replies on inbound SMS.
core_concepts: MessageTemplate: name + body text + merge field definitions, scoped to Profile
EmailTemplate: subject + HTML body + text fallback + attachments
QuickResponse: inbound SMS keyword → auto-reply template
TemplateAttachment: file in Azure Blob, referenced by template
design_decisions_for_green_ai: Template engine: Handlebars, Scriban, or simple {{field}} — pick one, document it
Attachments: part of template or added per-broadcast?
QuickResponse / auto-reply: green-ai v1 (yes/no) — requires Conversation domain first
Score too low (0.37) — needs dedicated discovery pass before phase 2 templates planning
not_to_copy: Templates across 4 tables with no common FK, QuickResponse mixed into SmsLog processing

name: Recipient Lists (Standard Receivers)
description: Pre-defined, profile-scoped lists of phone numbers. Used for employees, on-call teams, stakeholders. Can be synced from HR/AD via SCIM.
core_concepts: StandardReceiver: phone number + name, scoped to Profile
StdReceiverGroup: named list, also Profile-scoped
ExpandStandardReceiverGroupCommand: resolves group → individual numbers at send time
SCIM 2.0 inbound sync: HR system pushes updates → StandardReceiver records
design_decisions_for_green_ai: SCIM sync: green-ai v1 (no) or v2 (yes) — expensive to implement
Recipient lists: per-profile (same as sms-service) matches the Profile-as-primary-scope model
Phone number validation at add-time — use libphonenumber or E.164 validator
not_to_copy: SP-heavy group expansion, parallel StdReceiverGroup and StandardReceiver tables

name: Two-Way Communication (Conversation)
description: Inbound SMS matched to a profile phone number. Operator sees thread and can reply. SSE pushes updates in real time.
core_concepts: ConversationPhoneNumber: profile owns a dedicated inbound number
Conversation: thread per phone-number pair
SSE: ClientEventBackgroundService pushes new messages to browser
ProfileRole: SmsConversations must be enabled
design_decisions_for_green_ai: Dedicated inbound numbers = significant carrier cost — defer unless contractually required
SSE vs WebSocket vs SignalR — decide when deferred Conversation phase is planned
not_to_copy: ConversationMessage and SmsLog as separate tables with overlapping status tracking

name: Citizen Subscriptions
description: Citizens subscribe to address-based alerts without logging in (anonymous token). Utility companies send alerts to all subscribers of affected addresses.
core_concepts: AddressSubscription: citizen + address + channel preference
AnonymousTokenModel: subscription without citizen account
SupplyNumber: gas/water/electricity meter identifier
FtpSubscription: bulk subscription data from utility company via FTP
design_decisions_for_green_ai: Anonymous token auth: design security model carefully — JWTs with short TTL
FTP: modernize to SFTP or Azure Blob intake
Score 0.58 — needs discovery pass before phase 3 planning
not_to_copy: FTP-based data intake, mixed subscription and enrollment domain

name: Web Embed / Citizen Portal
description: Iframe widget for customer portal. Shows citizen's recent notifications. Authenticated via anonymous token.
core_concepts: WebMessage: message visible in portal embed
IframeRequest log: tracks embed usage by customer
AbsenceValidationStatus: checks if address is absent (summer house, vacation)
design_decisions_for_green_ai: Is iframe embed a green-ai product requirement?
AbsenceValidation: what external system provides this per country?
not_to_copy: IframeRequests as separate logging table, mixing absence validation with web message serving

name: Public API
description: Customers with the API ProfileRole trigger broadcasts programmatically. API key authentication. REST endpoints mirror a subset of the portal.
core_concepts: ProfileApiKey: bearer token scoped to Profile (not User)
API ProfileRole: must be enabled
Webhook receiver: carrier status callbacks (GatewayAPI, SendGrid, Infobip, E-boks)
design_decisions_for_green_ai: API key vs OAuth2 client credentials for M2M — prefer OAuth2 client_credentials
API versioning from day 1 (v1 namespace in URL)
API key hash: store SHA256(key), return plaintext only at creation
not_to_copy: API key stored plaintext in ProfileApiKeys

name: Customer Administration
description: Create customers, manage users, assign profiles, configure billing, manage SAML/SSO. Three admin tiers: SuperAdmin, CustomerAdmin, ProfileAdmin.
core_concepts: SuperAdmin: full system access (CustomerId in {1,9})
CustomerSetup UserRole: create/edit customers
ManageUsers UserRole: invite/remove users
ManageProfiles UserRole: create/edit profiles + capability flags
CloneProspectProductsToCustomer: copy template profile setup to new customer at onboarding
design_decisions_for_green_ai: Three-level admin hierarchy justified — SuperAdmin, CustomerAdmin, ProfileAdmin
CloneProspect: template gallery (UI-driven) or manual copy operation?
Blocked until SLICE-006 — see FOUNDATION_BUILD_PLAN.json
not_to_copy: CustomerUserRoleMappings naming confusion (rename to CustomerRolePolicies), ManageUsers as global UserRole

name: Reporting and Statistics
description: Post-delivery analytics: sent/delivered/failed per broadcast, per profile, per time period. Compliance retention: default 60 months (MonthToDeleteMessages).
core_concepts: SmsLogBroadcastStatisticStatus: aggregate per broadcast
StatisticsRequestLog: log of who ran which report
BenchmarkStatistic: performance comparison across broadcasts
MonthToDeleteMessages: per-customer retention period (default 60)
design_decisions_for_green_ai: Operational DB vs separate analytics store — avoid real-time aggregation on SmsLogs
GDPR: individual delivery logs deleted after MonthToDeleteMessages
Score 0.35 — DO NOT plan until score > 0.80
not_to_copy: SP-based reports, statistics computed on-the-fly from SmsLogs without aggregation table

name: Activity Log
description: Audit trail for operator actions. Stores translation keys (not resolved text). Resolved at read-time via ILocalizationService.
core_concepts: ActivityLog: header (type, objectId, dateCreated, userId)
ActivityLogEntry: ResourceKey + parameters — NOT resolved text
ActivityLogType enum: Broadcast, SubscriptionsImport, PositiveListsImport, StandardReceiversImport
Resolved at read time: ILocalizationService.GetString(entry.ResourceKey, languageId)
design_decisions_for_green_ai: Translation-key pattern is correct — keep in green-ai
ActivityLogType will grow — use extensible enum / string key from day 1
Index on (ResourceKey, DateCreated) from day 1 for compliance audit queries

descriptions.finance: e-conomic invoicing integration — sms-service billing, not a green-ai product concern
descriptions.benchmark: Internal performance comparison tool — if needed, build as analytics extension not a domain
